title: Code graph reuses per-file facts by content hash
summary: Kage code graph generation should cache per file extracted facts under .agent memory/code graph/file cache keyed by the file content hash. Unchanged files reuse cached file metadata, symbols, and imports; changed files m
body:
Kage code graph generation should cache per-file extracted facts under .agent_memory/code_graph/file-cache keyed by the file content hash. Unchanged files reuse cached file metadata, symbols, and imports; changed files miss the cache and regenerate facts. The cache is an internal performance layer, not durable memory, and global call edges still rebuild so cross-file relationships do not go stale. Manifest and metrics should expose cache_hits and cache_misses.
Evidence: Implemented in mcp/kernel.ts with FileCodeFacts cache helpers and CodeIndexManifest cache counters; covered by mcp/kernel.test.ts test 'code graph reuses per-file cached facts until source content changes'.
Verified by: npm test --prefix mcp
